verses.kjv: All the days of the afflicted are evil: but he that is of a merry heart hath a continual feast.
verses.bsb: All the days of the oppressed are bad, but a cheerful heart has a continual feast.

verses.kjv: Better is little with the fear of the Lord than great treasure and trouble therewith.
verses.bsb: Better a little with the fear of the LORD than great treasure with turmoil.

verses.kjv: Better is a dinner of herbs where love is, than a stalled ox and hatred therewith.
verses.bsb: Better a dish of vegetables where there is love than a fattened ox with hatred.

verses.kjv: A wrathful man stirreth up strife: but he that is slow to anger appeaseth strife.
verses.bsb: A hot-tempered man stirs up strife, but he who is slow to anger calms dispute.

verses.kjv: The way of the slothful man is as an hedge of thorns: but the way of the righteous is made plain.
verses.bsb: The way of the slacker is like a hedge of thorns, but the path of the upright is a highway.

verses.kjv: A wise son maketh a glad father: but a foolish man despiseth his mother.
verses.bsb: A wise son brings joy to his father, but a foolish man despises his mother.

verses.kjv: Folly is joy to him that is destitute of wisdom: but a man of understanding walketh uprightly.
verses.bsb: Folly is joy to one who lacks judgment, but a man of understanding walks a straight path.

verses.kjv: Without counsel purposes are disappointed: but in the multitude of counsellers they are established.
verses.bsb: Plans fail for lack of counsel, but with many advisers they succeed.

verses.kjv: A man hath joy by the answer of his mouth: and a word spoken in due season, how good is it!
verses.bsb: A man takes joy in a fitting reply— and how good is a timely word!

verses.kjv: The way of life is above to the wise, that he may depart from hell beneath.
verses.bsb: The path of life leads upward for the wise, that he may avoid going down to Sheol.

verses.kjv: The Lord will destroy the house of the proud: but he will establish the border of the widow.
verses.bsb: The LORD tears down the house of the proud, but He protects the boundaries of the widow.

verses.kjv: The thoughts of the wicked are an abomination to the Lord: but the words of the pure are pleasant words.
verses.bsb: The LORD detests the thoughts of the wicked, but the words of the pure are pleasant to Him.

verses.kjv: He that is greedy of gain troubleth his own house; but he that hateth gifts shall live.
verses.bsb: He who is greedy for unjust gain brings trouble on his household, but he who hates bribes will live.

verses.kjv: The heart of the righteous studieth to answer: but the mouth of the wicked poureth out evil things.
verses.bsb: The heart of the righteous ponders how to answer, but the mouth of the wicked blurts out evil.

verses.kjv: The Lord is far from the wicked: but he heareth the prayer of the righteous.
verses.bsb: The LORD is far from the wicked, but He hears the prayer of the righteous.

verses.kjv: The light of the eyes rejoiceth the heart: and a good report maketh the bones fat.
verses.bsb: The light of the eyes cheers the heart, and good news nourishes the bones.

verses.kjv: The ear that heareth the reproof of life abideth among the wise.
verses.bsb: He who listens to life-giving reproof will dwell among the wise.

verses.kjv: He that refuseth instruction despiseth his own soul: but he that heareth reproof getteth understanding.
verses.bsb: He who ignores discipline despises himself, but whoever heeds correction gains understanding.

verses.kjv: The fear of the Lord is the instruction of wisdom; and before honour is humility.
verses.bsb: The fear of the LORD is the instruction of wisdom, and humility comes before honor.

verses.kjv: THE preparations of the heart in man, and the answer of the tongue, is from the Lord.
verses.bsb: The plans of the heart belong to man, but the reply of the tongue is from the LORD.

verses.kjv: All the ways of a man are clean in his own eyes; but the Lord weigheth the spirits.
verses.bsb: All a man’s ways are pure in his own eyes, but his motives are weighed out by the LORD.

verses.kjv: Commit thy works unto the Lord, and thy thoughts shall be established.
verses.bsb: Commit your works to the LORD and your plans will be achieved.

verses.kjv: The Lord hath made all things for himself: yea, even the wicked for the day of evil.
verses.bsb: The LORD has made everything for His purpose— even the wicked for the day of disaster.

verses.kjv: Every one that is proud in heart is an abomination to the Lord: though hand join in hand, he shall not be unpunished.
verses.bsb: Everyone who is proud in heart is detestable to the LORD; be assured that he will not go unpunished.

verses.kjv: By mercy and truth iniquity is purged: and by the fear of the Lord men depart from evil.
verses.bsb: By loving devotion and faithfulness iniquity is atoned for, and by the fear of the LORD one turns aside from evil.

verses.kjv: When a man’s ways please the Lord, he maketh even his enemies to be at peace with him.
verses.bsb: When a man’s ways please the LORD, He makes even the man’s enemies live at peace with him.

verses.kjv: Better is a little with righteousness than great revenues without right.
verses.bsb: Better a little with righteousness than great gain with injustice.

verses.kjv: A man’s heart deviseth his way: but the Lord directeth his steps.
verses.bsb: A man’s heart plans his course, but the LORD determines his steps.

verses.kjv: A divine sentence is in the lips of the king: his mouth transgresseth not in judgment.
verses.bsb: A divine verdict is on the lips of a king; his mouth must not betray justice.

verses.kjv: A just weight and balance are the Lord’s: all the weights of the bag are his work.
verses.bsb: Honest scales and balances are from the LORD; all the weights in the bag are His concern.

verses.kjv: It is an abomination to kings to commit wickedness: for the throne is established by righteousness.
verses.bsb: Wicked behavior is detestable to kings, for a throne is established through righteousness.

verses.kjv: Righteous lips are the delight of kings; and they love him that speaketh right.
verses.bsb: Righteous lips are a king’s delight, and he who speaks honestly is beloved.

verses.kjv: The wrath of a king is as messengers of death: but a wise man will pacify it.
verses.bsb: The wrath of a king is a messenger of death, but a wise man will pacify it.

verses.kjv: In the light of the king’s countenance is life; and his favour is as a cloud of the latter rain.
verses.bsb: When a king’s face brightens, there is life; his favor is like a rain cloud in spring.

verses.kjv: How much better is it to get wisdom than gold! and to get understanding rather to be chosen than silver!
verses.bsb: How much better to acquire wisdom than gold! To gain understanding is more desirable than silver.

verses.kjv: The highway of the upright is to depart from evil: he that keepeth his way preserveth his soul.
verses.bsb: The highway of the upright leads away from evil; he who guards his way protects his life.

verses.kjv: Pride goeth before destruction, and an haughty spirit before a fall.
verses.bsb: Pride goes before destruction, and a haughty spirit before a fall.

verses.kjv: Better it is to be of an humble spirit with the lowly, than to divide the spoil with the proud.
verses.bsb: It is better to be lowly in spirit among the humble than to divide the spoil with the proud.

verses.kjv: He that handleth a matter wisely shall find good: and whoso trusteth in the Lord, happy is he.
verses.bsb: Whoever heeds instruction will find success, and blessed is he who trusts in the LORD.

verses.kjv: The wise in heart shall be called prudent: and the sweetness of the lips increaseth learning.
verses.bsb: The wise in heart are called discerning, and pleasant speech promotes instruction.

verses.kjv: Understanding is a wellspring of life unto him that hath it: but the instruction of fools is folly.
verses.bsb: Understanding is a fountain of life to its possessor, but the discipline of fools is folly.

verses.kjv: The heart of the wise teacheth his mouth, and addeth learning to his lips.
verses.bsb: The heart of the wise man instructs his mouth and adds persuasiveness to his lips.

verses.kjv: Pleasant words are as an honeycomb, sweet to the soul, and health to the bones.
verses.bsb: Pleasant words are a honeycomb, sweet to the soul and healing to the bones.

verses.kjv: There is a way that seemeth right unto a man, but the end thereof are the ways of death.
verses.bsb: There is a way that seems right to a man, but its end is the way of death.

verses.kjv: He that laboureth laboureth for himself; for his mouth craveth it of him.
verses.bsb: A worker’s appetite works for him because his hunger drives him onward.

verses.kjv: An ungodly man diggeth up evil: and in his lips there is as a burning fire.
verses.bsb: A worthless man digs up evil, and his speech is like a scorching fire.

verses.kjv: A froward man soweth strife: and a whisperer separateth chief friends.
verses.bsb: A perverse man spreads dissension, and a gossip divides close friends.

verses.kjv: A violent man enticeth his neighbour, and leadeth him into the way that is not good.
verses.bsb: A violent man entices his neighbor and leads him down a path that is not good.

verses.kjv: He shutteth his eyes to devise froward things: moving his lips he bringeth evil to pass.
verses.bsb: He who winks his eye devises perversity; he who purses his lips is bent on evil.

verses.kjv: The hoary head is a crown of glory, if it be found in the way of righteousness.
verses.bsb: Gray hair is a crown of glory; it is attained along the path of righteousness.

verses.kjv: He that is slow to anger is better than the mighty; and he that ruleth his spirit than he that taketh a city.
verses.bsb: He who is slow to anger is better than a warrior, and he who controls his temper is greater than one who captures a city.

verses.kjv: The lot is cast into the lap; but the whole disposing thereof is of the Lord.
verses.bsb: The lot is cast into the lap, but its every decision is from the LORD.

verses.kjv: BETTER is a dry morsel, and quietness therewith, than an house full of sacrifices with strife.
verses.bsb: Better a dry morsel in quietness than a house full of feasting with strife.

verses.kjv: A wise servant shall have rule over a son that causeth shame, and shall have part of the inheritance among the brethren.
verses.bsb: A wise servant will rule over a disgraceful son and share his inheritance as one of the brothers.

verses.kjv: The fining pot is for silver, and the furnace for gold: but the Lord trieth the hearts.
verses.bsb: A crucible for silver and a furnace for gold, but the LORD is the tester of hearts.

verses.kjv: A wicked doer giveth heed to false lips; and a liar giveth ear to a naughty tongue.
verses.bsb: A wicked man listens to evil lips; a liar gives ear to a destructive tongue.

verses.kjv: Whoso mocketh the poor reproacheth his Maker: and he that is glad at calamities shall not be unpunished.
verses.bsb: He who mocks the poor insults their Maker; whoever gloats over calamity will not go unpunished.

verses.kjv: Children’s children are the crown of old men; and the glory of children are their fathers.
verses.bsb: Grandchildren are the crown of the aged, and the glory of a son is his father.

verses.kjv: Excellent speech becometh not a fool: much less do lying lips a prince.
verses.bsb: Eloquent words are unfit for a fool; how much worse are lying lips to a ruler!

verses.kjv: A gift is as a precious stone in the eyes of him that hath it: whithersoever it turneth, it prospereth.
verses.bsb: A bribe is a charm to its giver; wherever he turns, he succeeds.

verses.kjv: He that covereth a transgression seeketh love; but he that repeateth a matter separateth very friends.
verses.bsb: Whoever conceals an offense promotes love, but he who brings it up separates friends.

verses.kjv: A reproof entereth more into a wise man than an hundred stripes into a fool.
verses.bsb: A rebuke cuts into a man of discernment deeper than a hundred lashes cut into a fool.

verses.kjv: An evil man seeketh only rebellion: therefore a cruel messenger shall be sent against him.
verses.bsb: An evil man seeks only rebellion; a cruel messenger will be sent against him.

verses.kjv: Let a bear robbed of her whelps meet a man, rather than a fool in his folly.
verses.bsb: It is better to meet a bear robbed of her cubs than a fool in his folly.

verses.kjv: Whoso rewardeth evil for good, evil shall not depart from his house.
verses.bsb: If anyone returns evil for good, evil will never leave his house.

verses.kjv: The beginning of strife is as when one letteth out water: therefore leave off contention, before it be meddled with.
verses.bsb: To start a quarrel is to release a flood; so abandon the dispute before it breaks out.

verses.kjv: He that justifieth the wicked, and he that condemneth the just, even they both are abomination to the Lord.
verses.bsb: Acquitting the guilty and condemning the righteous— both are detestable to the LORD.

verses.kjv: Wherefore is there a price in the hand of a fool to get wisdom, seeing he hath no heart to it?
verses.bsb: Why should the fool have money in his hand with no intention of buying wisdom?

verses.kjv: A friend loveth at all times, and a brother is born for adversity.
verses.bsb: A friend loves at all times, and a brother is born for adversity.

verses.kjv: A man void of understanding striketh hands, and becometh surety in the presence of his friend.
verses.bsb: A man lacking judgment strikes hands in pledge and puts up security for his neighbor.

verses.kjv: He loveth transgression that loveth strife: and he that exalteth his gate seeketh destruction.
verses.bsb: He who loves transgression loves strife; he who builds his gate high invites destruction.

verses.kjv: He that hath a froward heart findeth no good: and he that hath a perverse tongue falleth into mischief.
verses.bsb: The one with a perverse heart finds no good, and he whose tongue is deceitful falls into trouble.

verses.kjv: He that begetteth a fool doeth it to his sorrow: and the father of a fool hath no joy.
verses.bsb: A man fathers a fool to his own grief; the father of a fool has no joy.

verses.kjv: A merry heart doeth good like a medicine: but a broken spirit drieth the bones.
verses.bsb: A joyful heart is good medicine, but a broken spirit dries up the bones.

verses.kjv: A wicked man taketh a gift out of the bosom to pervert the ways of judgment.
verses.bsb: A wicked man takes a covert bribe to subvert the course of justice.

verses.kjv: Wisdom is before him that hath understanding; but the eyes of a fool are in the ends of the earth.
verses.bsb: Wisdom is the focus of the discerning, but the eyes of a fool wander to the ends of the earth.

verses.kjv: A foolish son is a grief to his father, and bitterness to her that bare him.
verses.bsb: A foolish son brings grief to his father and bitterness to her who bore him.

verses.kjv: Also to punish the just is not good, nor to strike princes for equity.
verses.bsb: It is surely not good to punish the innocent or to flog a noble for his honesty.

verses.kjv: He that hath knowledge spareth his words: and a man of understanding is of an excellent spirit.
verses.bsb: A man of knowledge restrains his words, and a man of understanding maintains a calm spirit.

verses.kjv: Even a fool, when he holdeth his peace, is counted wise: and he that shutteth his lips is esteemed a man of understanding.
verses.bsb: Even a fool is considered wise if he keeps silent, and discerning when he holds his tongue.

verses.kjv: THROUGH desire a man, having separated himself, seeketh and intermeddleth with all wisdom.
verses.bsb: He who isolates himself pursues selfish desires; he rebels against all sound judgment.

verses.kjv: A fool hath no delight in understanding, but that his heart may discover itself.
verses.bsb: A fool does not delight in understanding, but only in airing his opinions.

verses.kjv: When the wicked cometh, then cometh also contempt, and with ignominy reproach.
verses.bsb: With a wicked man comes contempt as well, and shame is accompanied by disgrace.

verses.kjv: The words of a man’s mouth are as deep waters, and the wellspring of wisdom as a flowing brook.
verses.bsb: The words of a man’s mouth are deep waters; the fountain of wisdom is a bubbling brook.

verses.kjv: It is not good to accept the person of the wicked, to overthrow the righteous in judgment.
verses.bsb: Showing partiality to the wicked is not good, nor is depriving the innocent of justice.

verses.kjv: A fool’s lips enter into contention, and his mouth calleth for strokes.
verses.bsb: A fool’s lips bring him strife, and his mouth invites a beating.

verses.kjv: A fool’s mouth is his destruction, and his lips are the snare of his soul.
verses.bsb: A fool’s mouth is his ruin, and his lips are a snare to his soul.

verses.kjv: The words of a talebearer are as wounds, and they go down into the innermost parts of the belly.
verses.bsb: The words of a gossip are like choice morsels that go down into the inmost being.

verses.kjv: He also that is slothful in his work is brother to him that is a great waster.
verses.bsb: Whoever is slothful in his work is brother to him who destroys.

verses.kjv: The name of the Lord is a strong tower: the righteous runneth into it, and is safe.
verses.bsb: The name of the LORD is a strong tower; the righteous run to it and are safe.

verses.kjv: The rich man’s wealth is his strong city, and as an high wall in his own conceit.
verses.bsb: A rich man’s wealth is his fortified city; it is like a high wall in his imagination.

verses.kjv: Before destruction the heart of man is haughty, and before honour is humility.
verses.bsb: Before his downfall a man’s heart is proud, but humility comes before honor.

verses.kjv: He that answereth a matter before he heareth it, it is folly and shame unto him.
verses.bsb: He who answers a matter before he hears it— this is folly and disgrace to him.

verses.kjv: The spirit of a man will sustain his infirmity; but a wounded spirit who can bear?
verses.bsb: The spirit of a man can endure his sickness, but who can survive a broken spirit?

verses.kjv: The heart of the prudent getteth knowledge; and the ear of the wise seeketh knowledge.
verses.bsb: The heart of the discerning acquires knowledge, and the ear of the wise seeks it out.

verses.kjv: A man’s gift maketh room for him, and bringeth him before great men.
verses.bsb: A man’s gift opens doors for him, and brings him before great men.

verses.kjv: He that is first in his own cause seemeth just; but his neighbour cometh and searcheth him.
verses.bsb: The first to state his case seems right until another comes and cross-examines him.

verses.kjv: The lot causeth contentions to cease, and parteth between the mighty.
verses.bsb: Casting the lot ends quarrels and separates strong opponents.

verses.kjv: A brother offended is harder to be won than a strong city: and their contentions are like the bars of a castle.
verses.bsb: An offended brother is harder to win than a fortified city, and disputes are like the bars of a castle.

verses.kjv: A man’s belly shall be satisfied with the fruit of his mouth; and with the increase of his lips shall he be filled.
verses.bsb: From the fruit of his mouth a man’s belly is filled; with the harvest from his lips he is satisfied.

verses.kjv: Death and life are in the power of the tongue: and they that love it shall eat the fruit thereof.
verses.bsb: Life and death are in the power of the tongue, and those who love it will eat its fruit.

verses.kjv: Whoso findeth a wife findeth a good thing, and obtaineth favour of the Lord.
verses.bsb: He who finds a wife finds a good thing and obtains favor from the LORD.

verses.kjv: The poor useth intreaties; but the rich answereth roughly.
verses.bsb: The poor man pleads for mercy, but the rich man answers harshly.

verses.kjv: A man that hath friends must shew himself friendly: and there is a friend that sticketh closer than a brother.
verses.bsb: A man of many companions may come to ruin, but there is a friend who stays closer than a brother.

verses.kjv: BETTER is the poor that walketh in his integrity, than he that is perverse in his lips, and is a fool.
verses.bsb: Better a poor man who walks with integrity than a fool whose lips are perverse.

verses.kjv: Also, that the soul be without knowledge, it is not good; and he that hasteth with his feet sinneth.
verses.bsb: Even zeal is no good without knowledge, and he who hurries his footsteps misses the mark.

verses.kjv: The foolishness of man perverteth his way: and his heart fretteth against the Lord.
verses.bsb: A man’s own folly subverts his way, yet his heart rages against the LORD.

verses.kjv: Wealth maketh many friends; but the poor is separated from his neighbour.
verses.bsb: Wealth attracts many friends, but a poor man is deserted by his friend.

verses.kjv: A false witness shall not be unpunished, and he that speaketh lies shall not escape.
verses.bsb: A false witness will not go unpunished, and one who utters lies will not escape.

verses.kjv: Many will intreat the favour of the prince: and every man is a friend to him that giveth gifts.
verses.bsb: Many seek the favor of the prince, and everyone is a friend of the gift giver.

verses.kjv: All the brethren of the poor do hate him: how much more do his friends go far from him? he pursueth them with words, yet they are wanting to him.
verses.bsb: All the brothers of a poor man hate him— how much more do his friends avoid him! He may pursue them with pleading, but they are nowhere to be found.

verses.kjv: He that getteth wisdom loveth his own soul: he that keepeth understanding shall find good.
verses.bsb: He who acquires wisdom loves himself; one who safeguards understanding will find success.

verses.kjv: A false witness shall not be unpunished, and he that speaketh lies shall perish.
verses.bsb: A false witness will not go unpunished, and one who pours out lies will perish.

verses.kjv: Delight is not seemly for a fool; much less for a servant to have rule over princes.
verses.bsb: Luxury is unseemly for a fool— how much worse for a slave to rule over princes!

verses.kjv: The discretion of a man deferreth his anger; and it is his glory to pass over a transgression.
verses.bsb: A man’s insight gives him patience, and his virtue is to overlook an offense.

verses.kjv: The king’s wrath is as the roaring of a lion; but his favour is as dew upon the grass.
verses.bsb: A king’s rage is like the roar of a lion, but his favor is like dew on the grass.

verses.kjv: A foolish son is the calamity of his father: and the contentions of a wife are a continual dropping.
verses.bsb: A foolish son is his father’s ruin, and a quarrelsome wife is like a constant dripping.

verses.kjv: House and riches are the inheritance of fathers: and a prudent wife is from the Lord.
verses.bsb: Houses and wealth are inherited from fathers, but a prudent wife is from the LORD.

verses.kjv: Slothfulness casteth into a deep sleep; and an idle soul shall suffer hunger.
verses.bsb: Laziness brings on deep sleep, and an idle soul will suffer hunger.

verses.kjv: He that keepeth the commandment keepeth his own soul; but he that despiseth his ways shall die.
verses.bsb: He who keeps a commandment preserves his soul, but he who is careless in his ways will die.

verses.kjv: He that hath pity upon the poor lendeth unto the Lord; and that which he hath given will he pay him again.
verses.bsb: Kindness to the poor is a loan to the LORD, and He will repay the lender.

verses.kjv: Chasten thy son while there is hope, and let not thy soul spare for his crying.
verses.bsb: Discipline your son, for in that there is hope; do not be party to his death.

verses.kjv: A man of great wrath shall suffer punishment: for if thou deliver him, yet thou must do it again.
verses.bsb: A man of great anger must pay the penalty; if you rescue him, you will have to do so again.

verses.kjv: Hear counsel, and receive instruction, that thou mayest be wise in thy latter end.
verses.bsb: Listen to counsel and accept discipline, that you may be wise the rest of your days.

verses.kjv: There are many devices in a man’s heart; nevertheless the counsel of the Lord, that shall stand.
verses.bsb: Many plans are in a man’s heart, but the purpose of the LORD will prevail.

verses.kjv: The desire of a man is his kindness: and a poor man is better than a liar.
verses.bsb: The desire of a man is loving devotion; better to be poor than a liar.

verses.kjv: The fear of the Lord tendeth to life: and he that hath it shall abide satisfied; he shall not be visited with evil.
verses.bsb: The fear of the LORD leads to life, that one may rest content, without visitation from harm.

verses.kjv: A slothful man hideth his hand in his bosom, and will not so much as bring it to his mouth again.
verses.bsb: The slacker buries his hand in the dish; he will not even bring it back to his mouth.

verses.kjv: Smite a scorner, and the simple will beware: and reprove one that hath understanding, and he will understand knowledge.
verses.bsb: Strike a mocker, and the simple will beware; rebuke the discerning man, and he will gain knowledge.

verses.kjv: He that wasteth his father, and chaseth away his mother, is a son that causeth shame, and bringeth reproach.
verses.bsb: He who assaults his father or evicts his mother is a son who brings shame and disgrace.

verses.kjv: Cease, my son, to hear the instruction that causeth to err from the words of knowledge.
verses.bsb: If you cease to hear instruction, my son, you will stray from the words of knowledge.

verses.kjv: An ungodly witness scorneth judgment: and the mouth of the wicked devoureth iniquity.
verses.bsb: A corrupt witness mocks justice, and a wicked mouth swallows iniquity.

verses.kjv: Judgments are prepared for scorners, and stripes for the back of fools.
verses.bsb: Judgments are prepared for mockers, and beatings for the backs of fools.

verses.kjv: WINE is a mocker, strong drink is raging: and whosoever is deceived thereby is not wise.
verses.bsb: Wine is a mocker, strong drink is a brawler, and whoever is led astray by them is not wise.

verses.kjv: The fear of a king is as the roaring of a lion: whoso provoketh him to anger sinneth against his own soul.
verses.bsb: The terror of a king is like the roar of a lion; whoever provokes him forfeits his own life.

verses.kjv: It is an honour for a man to cease from strife: but every fool will be meddling.
verses.bsb: It is honorable for a man to resolve a dispute, but any fool will quarrel.

verses.kjv: The sluggard will not plow by reason of the cold; therefore shall he beg in harvest, and have nothing.
verses.bsb: The slacker does not plow in season; at harvest time he looks, but nothing is there.

verses.kjv: Counsel in the heart of man is like deep water; but a man of understanding will draw it out.
verses.bsb: The intentions of a man’s heart are deep waters, but a man of understanding draws them out.

verses.kjv: Most men will proclaim every one his own goodness: but a faithful man who can find?
verses.bsb: Many a man proclaims his loving devotion, but who can find a trustworthy man?

verses.kjv: The just man walketh in his integrity: his children are blessed after him.
verses.bsb: The righteous man walks with integrity; blessed are his children after him.

verses.kjv: A king that sitteth in the throne of judgment scattereth away all evil with his eyes.
verses.bsb: A king who sits on a throne to judge sifts out all evil with his eyes.

verses.kjv: Who can say, I have made my heart clean, I am pure from my sin?
verses.bsb: Who can say, “I have kept my heart pure; I am cleansed from my sin”?

verses.kjv: Divers weights, and divers measures, both of them are alike abomination to the Lord.
verses.bsb: Differing weights and unequal measures— both are detestable to the LORD.

verses.kjv: Even a child is known by his doings, whether his work be pure, and whether it be right.
verses.bsb: Even a young man is known by his actions— whether his conduct is pure and upright.

verses.kjv: The hearing ear, and the seeing eye, the Lord hath made even both of them.
verses.bsb: Ears that hear and eyes that see— the LORD has made them both.

verses.kjv: Love not sleep, lest thou come to poverty; open thine eyes, and thou shalt be satisfied with bread.
verses.bsb: Do not love sleep, or you will grow poor; open your eyes, and you will have plenty of food.

verses.kjv: It is naught, it is naught, saith the buyer: but when he is gone his way, then he boasteth.
verses.bsb: “Worthless, worthless!” says the buyer, but on the way out, he gloats.

verses.kjv: There is gold, and a multitude of rubies: but the lips of knowledge are a precious jewel.
verses.bsb: There is an abundance of gold and rubies, but lips of knowledge are a rare treasure.

verses.kjv: Take his garment that is surety for a stranger: and take a pledge of him for a strange woman.
verses.bsb: Take the garment of the one who posts security for a stranger; get collateral if it is for a foreigner.

verses.kjv: Bread of deceit is sweet to a man; but afterwards his mouth shall be filled with gravel.
verses.bsb: Food gained by fraud is sweet to a man, but later his mouth is full of gravel.

verses.kjv: Every purpose is established by counsel: and with good advice make war.
verses.bsb: Set plans by consultation, and wage war under sound guidance.

verses.kjv: He that goeth about as a talebearer revealeth secrets: therefore meddle not with him that flattereth with his lips.
verses.bsb: He who reveals secrets is a constant gossip; avoid the one who babbles with his lips.

verses.kjv: Whoso curseth his father or his mother, his lamp shall be put out in obscure darkness.
verses.bsb: Whoever curses his father or mother, his lamp will be extinguished in deepest darkness.

verses.kjv: An inheritance may be gotten hastily at the beginning; but the end thereof shall not be blessed.
verses.bsb: An inheritance gained quickly will not be blessed in the end.

verses.kjv: Say not thou, I will recompense evil; but wait on the Lord, and he shall save thee.
verses.bsb: Do not say, “I will avenge this evil!” Wait on the LORD, and He will save you.

verses.kjv: Divers weights are an abomination unto the Lord; and a false balance is not good.
verses.bsb: Unequal weights are detestable to the LORD, and dishonest scales are no good.

verses.kjv: Man’s goings are of the Lord; how can a man then understand his own way?
verses.bsb: A man’s steps are from the LORD, so how can anyone understand his own way?

verses.kjv: It is a snare to the man who devoureth that which is holy, and after vows to make inquiry.
verses.bsb: It is a trap for a man to dedicate something rashly, only later to reconsider his vows.

verses.kjv: A wise king scattereth the wicked, and bringeth the wheel over them.
verses.bsb: A wise king separates out the wicked and drives the threshing wheel over them.

verses.kjv: The spirit of man is the candle of the Lord, searching all the inward parts of the belly.
verses.bsb: The spirit of a man is the lamp of the LORD, searching out his inmost being.

verses.kjv: Mercy and truth preserve the king: and his throne is upholden by mercy.
verses.bsb: Loving devotion and faithfulness preserve a king; by these he maintains his throne.

verses.kjv: The glory of young men is their strength: and the beauty of old men is the gray head.
verses.bsb: The glory of young men is their strength, and gray hair is the splendor of the old.

verses.kjv: The blueness of a wound cleanseth away evil: so do stripes the inward parts of the belly.
verses.bsb: Lashes and wounds scour evil, and beatings cleanse the inmost parts.

verses.kjv: THE king’s heart is in the hand of the Lord, as the rivers of water: he turneth it whithersoever he will.
verses.bsb: The king’s heart is a waterway in the hand of the LORD; He directs it where He pleases.

verses.kjv: Every way of a man is right in his own eyes: but the Lord pondereth the hearts.
verses.bsb: All a man’s ways seem right to him, but the LORD weighs the heart.

verses.kjv: To do justice and judgment is more acceptable to the Lord than sacrifice.
verses.bsb: To do righteousness and justice is more desirable to the LORD than sacrifice.

verses.kjv: An high look, and a proud heart, and the plowing of the wicked, is sin.
verses.bsb: Haughty eyes and a proud heart— the guides of the wicked—are sin.

verses.kjv: The thoughts of the diligent tend only to plenteousness; but of every one that is hasty only to want.
verses.bsb: The plans of the diligent bring plenty, as surely as haste leads to poverty.

verses.kjv: The getting of treasures by a lying tongue is a vanity tossed to and fro of them that seek death.
verses.bsb: Making a fortune by a lying tongue is a vanishing mist, a deadly pursuit.

verses.kjv: The robbery of the wicked shall destroy them; because they refuse to do judgment.
verses.bsb: The violence of the wicked will sweep them away because they refuse to do what is just.

verses.kjv: The way of man is froward and strange: but as for the pure, his work is right.
verses.bsb: The way of a guilty man is crooked, but the conduct of the innocent is upright.

verses.kjv: It is better to dwell in a corner of the housetop, than with a brawling woman in a wide house.
verses.bsb: Better to live on a corner of the roof than to share a house with a quarrelsome wife.

verses.kjv: The soul of the wicked desireth evil: his neighbour findeth no favour in his eyes.
verses.bsb: The soul of the wicked man craves evil; his neighbor finds no favor in his eyes.

verses.kjv: When the scorner is punished, the simple is made wise: and when the wise is instructed, he receiveth knowledge.
verses.bsb: When a mocker is punished, the simple gain wisdom; and when a wise man is instructed, he acquires knowledge.

verses.kjv: The righteous man wisely considereth the house of the wicked: but God overthroweth the wicked for their wickedness.
verses.bsb: The Righteous One considers the house of the wicked and brings the wicked to ruin.

verses.kjv: Whoso stoppeth his ears at the cry of the poor, he also shall cry himself, but shall not be heard.
verses.bsb: Whoever shuts his ears to the cry of the poor, he too shall cry out and receive no answer.

verses.kjv: A gift in secret pacifieth anger: and a reward in the bosom strong wrath.
verses.bsb: A gift in secret soothes anger, and a covert bribe pacifies great wrath.

verses.kjv: It is joy to the just to do judgment: but destruction shall be to the workers of iniquity.
verses.bsb: Justice executed is a joy to the righteous, but a terror to the workers of iniquity.

verses.kjv: The man that wandereth out of the way of understanding shall remain in the congregation of the dead.
verses.bsb: The man who strays from the path of understanding will rest in the assembly of the dead.

verses.kjv: He that loveth pleasure shall be a poor man: he that loveth wine and oil shall not be rich.
verses.bsb: He who loves pleasure will become poor; the one who loves wine and oil will never be rich.

verses.kjv: The wicked shall be a ransom for the righteous, and the transgressor for the upright.
verses.bsb: The wicked become a ransom for the righteous, and the faithless for the upright.

verses.kjv: It is better to dwell in the wilderness, than with a contentious and an angry woman.
verses.bsb: Better to live in the desert than with a contentious and ill-tempered wife.

verses.kjv: There is treasure to be desired and oil in the dwelling of the wise; but a foolish man spendeth it up.
verses.bsb: Precious treasures and oil are in the dwelling of the wise, but a foolish man consumes them.

verses.kjv: He that followeth after righteousness and mercy findeth life, righteousness, and honour.
verses.bsb: He who pursues righteousness and loving devotion finds life, righteousness, and honor.

verses.kjv: A wise man scaleth the city of the mighty, and casteth down the strength of the confidence thereof.
verses.bsb: A wise man scales the city of the mighty and pulls down the stronghold in which they trust.

verses.kjv: Whoso keepeth his mouth and his tongue keepeth his soul from troubles.
verses.bsb: He who guards his mouth and tongue keeps his soul from distress.

verses.kjv: Proud and haughty scorner is his name, who dealeth in proud wrath.
verses.bsb: Mocker is the name of the proud and arrogant man— of him who acts with excessive pride.

verses.kjv: The desire of the slothful killeth him; for his hands refuse to labour.
verses.bsb: The craving of the slacker kills him because his hands refuse to work.

verses.kjv: He coveteth greedily all the day long: but the righteous giveth and spareth not.
verses.bsb: All day long he covets more, but the righteous give without restraint.

verses.kjv: The sacrifice of the wicked is abomination: how much more, when he bringeth it with a wicked mind?
verses.bsb: The sacrifice of the wicked is detestable— how much more so when brought with ill intent!

verses.kjv: A false witness shall perish: but the man that heareth speaketh constantly.
verses.bsb: A lying witness will perish, but the man who listens to truth will speak forever.

verses.kjv: A wicked man hardeneth his face: but as for the upright, he directeth his way.
verses.bsb: A wicked man hardens his face, but the upright man makes his way sure.

verses.kjv: There is no wisdom nor understanding nor counsel against the Lord.
verses.bsb: There is no wisdom, no understanding, no counsel that can prevail against the LORD.

verses.kjv: The horse is prepared against the day of battle: but safety is of the Lord.
verses.bsb: A horse is prepared for the day of battle, but victory is of the LORD.

verses.kjv: A GOOD name is rather to be chosen than great riches, and loving favour rather than silver and gold.
verses.bsb: A good name is more desirable than great riches; favor is better than silver and gold.

verses.kjv: The rich and poor meet together: the Lord is the maker of them all.
verses.bsb: The rich and the poor have this in common: The LORD is Maker of them all.

verses.kjv: A prudent man foreseeth the evil, and hideth himself: but the simple pass on, and are punished.
verses.bsb: The prudent see danger and take cover, but the simple keep going and suffer the consequences.

verses.kjv: By humility and the fear of the Lord are riches, and honour, and life.
verses.bsb: The rewards of humility and the fear of the LORD are wealth and honor and life.

verses.kjv: Thorns and snares are in the way of the froward: he that doth keep his soul shall be far from them.
verses.bsb: Thorns and snares lie on the path of the perverse; he who guards his soul stays far from them.

verses.kjv: Train up a child in the way he should go: and when he is old, he will not depart from it.
verses.bsb: Train up a child in the way he should go, and when he is old he will not depart from it.

verses.kjv: The rich ruleth over the poor, and the borrower is servant to the lender.
verses.bsb: The rich rule over the poor, and the borrower is slave to the lender.

verses.kjv: He that soweth iniquity shall reap vanity: and the rod of his anger shall fail.
verses.bsb: He who sows injustice will reap disaster, and the rod of his fury will be destroyed.

verses.kjv: He that hath a bountiful eye shall be blessed; for he giveth of his bread to the poor.
verses.bsb: A generous man will be blessed, for he shares his bread with the poor.

verses.kjv: Cast out the scorner, and contention shall go out; yea, strife and reproach shall cease.
verses.bsb: Drive out the mocker, and conflict will depart; even quarreling and insults will cease.

verses.kjv: He that loveth pureness of heart, for the grace of his lips the king shall be his friend.
verses.bsb: He who loves a pure heart and gracious lips will have the king for a friend.

verses.kjv: The eyes of the Lord preserve knowledge, and he overthroweth the words of the transgressor.
verses.bsb: The LORD’s eyes keep watch over knowledge, but He frustrates the words of the faithless.

verses.kjv: The slothful man saith, There is a lion without, I shall be slain in the streets.
verses.bsb: The slacker says, “There is a lion outside! I will be slain in the streets!”

verses.kjv: The mouth of strange women is a deep pit: he that is abhorred of the Lord shall fall therein.
verses.bsb: The mouth of an adulteress is a deep pit; he who is under the wrath of the LORD will fall into it.

verses.kjv: Foolishness is bound in the heart of a child; but the rod of correction shall drive it far from him.
verses.bsb: Foolishness is bound up in the heart of a child, but the rod of discipline drives it far from him.

verses.kjv: He that oppresseth the poor to increase his riches, and he that giveth to the rich, shall surely come to want.
verses.bsb: Oppressing the poor to enrich oneself or giving gifts to the rich will surely lead to poverty.

verses.kjv: Bow down thine ear, and hear the words of the wise, and apply thine heart unto my knowledge.
verses.bsb: Incline your ear and hear the words of the wise— apply your mind to my knowledge—

verses.kjv: For it is a pleasant thing if thou keep them within thee; they shall withal be fitted in thy lips.
verses.bsb: for it is pleasing when you keep them within you and they are constantly on your lips.

verses.kjv: That thy trust may be in the Lord, I have made known to thee this day, even to thee.
verses.bsb: So that your trust may be in the LORD, I instruct you today—yes, you.

verses.kjv: Have not I written to thee excellent things in counsels and knowledge,
verses.bsb: Have I not written for you thirty sayings about counsel and knowledge,

verses.kjv: That I might make thee know the certainty of the words of truth; that thou mightest answer the words of truth to them that send unto thee?
verses.bsb: to show you true and reliable words, that you may soundly answer those who sent you?

verses.kjv: Rob not the poor, because he is poor: neither oppress the afflicted in the gate:
verses.bsb: Do not rob a poor man because he is poor, and do not crush the afflicted at the gate,

verses.kjv: For the Lord will plead their cause, and spoil the soul of those that spoiled them.
verses.bsb: for the LORD will take up their case and will plunder those who rob them.

verses.kjv: Make no friendship with an angry man; and with a furious man thou shalt not go:
verses.bsb: Do not make friends with an angry man, and do not associate with a hot-tempered man,

verses.kjv: Lest thou learn his ways, and get a snare to thy soul.
verses.bsb: or you may learn his ways and entangle yourself in a snare.

verses.kjv: Be not thou one of them that strike hands, or of them that are sureties for debts.
verses.bsb: Do not be one who gives pledges, who puts up security for debts.

verses.kjv: If thou hast nothing to pay, why should he take away thy bed from under thee?
verses.bsb: If you have nothing with which to pay, why should your bed be taken from under you?

verses.kjv: Remove not the ancient landmark, which thy fathers have set.
verses.bsb: Do not move an ancient boundary stone which your fathers have placed.

verses.kjv: Seest thou a man diligent in his business? he shall stand before kings; he shall not stand before mean men.
verses.bsb: Do you see a man skilled in his work? He will be stationed in the presence of kings; he will not stand before obscure men.

verses.kjv: WHEN thou sittest to eat with a ruler, consider diligently what is before thee:
verses.bsb: When you sit down to dine with a ruler, consider carefully what is set before you,

verses.kjv: And put a knife to thy throat, if thou be a man given to appetite.
verses.bsb: and put a knife to your throat if you possess a great appetite.

verses.kjv: Be not desirous of his dainties: for they are deceitful meat.
verses.bsb: Do not crave his delicacies, for that food is deceptive.

verses.kjv: Labour not to be rich: cease from thine own wisdom.
verses.bsb: Do not wear yourself out to get rich; be wise enough to restrain yourself.

verses.kjv: Wilt thou set thine eyes upon that which is not? for riches certainly make themselves wings; they fly away as an eagle toward heaven.
verses.bsb: When you glance at wealth, it disappears, for it makes wings for itself and flies like an eagle to the sky.

verses.kjv: Eat thou not the bread of him that hath an evil eye, neither desire thou his dainty meats:
verses.bsb: Do not eat the bread of a stingy man, and do not crave his delicacies;

verses.kjv: For as he thinketh in his heart, so is he: Eat and drink, saith he to thee; but his heart is not with thee.
verses.bsb: for he is keeping track, inwardly counting the cost. “Eat and drink,” he says to you, but his heart is not with you.

verses.kjv: The morsel which thou hast eaten shalt thou vomit up, and lose thy sweet words.
verses.bsb: You will vomit up what little you have eaten and waste your pleasant words.

verses.kjv: Speak not in the ears of a fool: for he will despise the wisdom of thy words.
verses.bsb: Do not speak to a fool, for he will despise the wisdom of your words.

verses.kjv: Remove not the old landmark; and enter not into the fields of the fatherless:
verses.bsb: Do not move an ancient boundary stone or encroach on the fields of the fatherless,

verses.kjv: For their redeemer is mighty; he shall plead their cause with thee.
verses.bsb: for their Redeemer is strong; He will take up their case against you.

verses.kjv: Apply thine heart unto instruction, and thine ears to the words of knowledge.
verses.bsb: Apply your heart to instruction and your ears to words of knowledge.

verses.kjv: Withhold not correction from the child: for if thou beatest him with the rod, he shall not die.
verses.bsb: Do not withhold discipline from a child; although you strike him with a rod, he will not die.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt beat him with the rod, and shalt deliver his soul from hell.
verses.bsb: Strike him with a rod, and you will deliver his soul from Sheol.

verses.kjv: My son, if thine heart be wise, my heart shall rejoice, even mine.
verses.bsb: My son, if your heart is wise, my own heart will indeed rejoice.

verses.kjv: Yea, my reins shall rejoice, when thy lips speak right things.
verses.bsb: My inmost being will rejoice when your lips speak what is right.

verses.kjv: Let not thine heart envy sinners: but be thou in the fear of the Lord all the day long.
verses.bsb: Do not let your heart envy sinners, but always continue in the fear of the LORD.

verses.kjv: For surely there is an end; and thine expectation shall not be cut off.
verses.bsb: For surely there is a future, and your hope will not be cut off.

verses.kjv: Hear thou, my son, and be wise, and guide thine heart in the way.
verses.bsb: Listen, my son, and be wise, and guide your heart on the right course.

verses.kjv: Be not among winebibbers; among riotous eaters of flesh:
verses.bsb: Do not join those who drink too much wine or gorge themselves on meat.

verses.kjv: For the drunkard and the glutton shall come to poverty: and drowsiness shall clothe a man with rags.
verses.bsb: For the drunkard and the glutton will come to poverty, and drowsiness will clothe them in rags.

verses.kjv: Hearken unto thy father that begat thee, and despise not thy mother when she is old.
verses.bsb: Listen to your father who gave you life, and do not despise your mother when she is old.

verses.kjv: Buy the truth, and sell it not; also wisdom, and instruction, and understanding.
verses.bsb: Invest in truth and never sell it— in wisdom and instruction and understanding.

verses.kjv: The father of the righteous shall greatly rejoice: and he that begetteth a wise child shall have joy of him.
verses.bsb: The father of a righteous man will greatly rejoice, and he who fathers a wise son will delight in him.

verses.kjv: Thy father and thy mother shall be glad, and she that bare thee shall rejoice.
verses.bsb: May your father and mother be glad, and may she who gave you birth rejoice!

verses.kjv: My son, give me thine heart, and let thine eyes observe my ways.
verses.bsb: My son, give me your heart, and let your eyes delight in my ways.

verses.kjv: For a whore is a deep ditch; and a strange woman is a narrow pit.
verses.bsb: For a prostitute is a deep pit, and an adulteress is a narrow well.

verses.kjv: She also lieth in wait as for a prey, and increaseth the transgressors among men.
verses.bsb: Like a robber she lies in wait and multiplies the faithless among men.

verses.kjv: Who hath woe? who hath sorrow? who hath contentions? who hath babbling? who hath wounds without cause? who hath redness of eyes?
verses.bsb: Who has woe? Who has sorrow? Who has contentions? Who has complaints? Who has needless wounds? Who has bloodshot eyes?

verses.kjv: They that tarry long at the wine; they that go to seek mixed wine.
verses.bsb: Those who linger over wine, who go to taste mixed drinks.

verses.kjv: Look not thou upon the wine when it is red, when it giveth his colour in the cup, when it moveth itself aright.
verses.bsb: Do not gaze at wine while it is red, when it sparkles in the cup and goes down smoothly.

verses.kjv: At the last it biteth like a serpent, and stingeth like an adder.
verses.bsb: In the end it bites like a snake and stings like a viper.

verses.kjv: Thine eyes shall behold strange women, and thine heart shall utter perverse things.
verses.bsb: Your eyes will see strange things, and your mind will utter perversities.

verses.kjv: Yea, thou shalt be as he that lieth down in the midst of the sea, or as he that lieth upon the top of a mast.
verses.bsb: You will be like one sleeping on the high seas or lying on the top of a mast:

verses.kjv: They have stricken me, shalt thou say, and I was not sick; they have beaten me, and I felt it not: when shall I awake? I will seek it yet again.
verses.bsb: “They struck me, but I feel no pain! They beat me, but I did not know it! When can I wake up to search for another drink?”

verses.kjv: BE not thou envious against evil men, neither desire to be with them.
verses.bsb: Do not envy wicked men or desire their company;

verses.kjv: For their heart studieth destruction, and their lips talk of mischief.
verses.bsb: for their hearts devise violence, and their lips declare trouble.

verses.kjv: Through wisdom is an house builded; and by understanding it is established:
verses.bsb: By wisdom a house is built and by understanding it is established;

verses.kjv: And by knowledge shall the chambers be filled with all precious and pleasant riches.
verses.bsb: through knowledge its rooms are filled with every precious and beautiful treasure.

verses.kjv: A wise man is strong; yea, a man of knowledge increaseth strength.
verses.bsb: A wise man is strong, and a man of knowledge enhances his strength.

verses.kjv: For by wise counsel thou shalt make thy war: and in multitude of counsellers there is safety.
verses.bsb: Only with sound guidance should you wage war, and victory lies in a multitude of counselors.

verses.kjv: Wisdom is too high for a fool: he openeth not his mouth in the gate.
verses.bsb: Wisdom is too high for a fool; he does not open his mouth in the meeting place.

verses.kjv: He that deviseth to do evil shall be called a mischievous person.
verses.bsb: He who plots evil will be called a schemer.

verses.kjv: The thought of foolishness is sin: and the scorner is an abomination to men.
verses.bsb: A foolish scheme is sin, and a mocker is detestable to men.

verses.kjv: If thou faint in the day of adversity, thy strength is small.
verses.bsb: If you faint in the day of distress, how small is your strength!

verses.kjv: If thou forbear to deliver them that are drawn unto death, and those that are ready to be slain;
verses.bsb: Rescue those being led away to death, and restrain those stumbling toward the slaughter.

verses.kjv: If thou sayest, Behold, we knew it not; doth not he that pondereth the heart consider it? and he that keepeth thy soul, doth not he know it? and shall not he render to every man according to his works?
verses.bsb: If you say, “Behold, we did not know about this,” does not He who weighs hearts consider it? Does not the One who guards your life know? Will He not repay a man according to his deeds?

verses.kjv: My son, eat thou honey, because it is good; and the honeycomb, which is sweet to thy taste:
verses.bsb: Eat honey, my son, for it is good, and the honeycomb is sweet to your taste.

verses.kjv: So shall the knowledge of wisdom be unto thy soul: when thou hast found it, then there shall be a reward, and thy expectation shall not be cut off.
verses.bsb: Know therefore that wisdom is sweet to your soul. If you find it, there is a future for you, and your hope will never be cut off.

verses.kjv: Lay not wait, O wicked man, against the dwelling of the righteous; spoil not his resting place:
verses.bsb: Do not lie in wait, O wicked man, near the dwelling of the righteous; do not destroy his resting place.

verses.kjv: For a just man falleth seven times, and riseth up again: but the wicked shall fall into mischief.
verses.bsb: For though a righteous man may fall seven times, he still gets up; but the wicked stumble in bad times.

verses.kjv: Rejoice not when thine enemy falleth, and let not thine heart be glad when he stumbleth:
verses.bsb: Do not gloat when your enemy falls, and do not let your heart rejoice when he stumbles,

verses.kjv: Lest the Lord see it, and it displease him, and he turn away his wrath from him.
verses.bsb: or the LORD will see and disapprove, and turn His wrath away from him.

verses.kjv: Fret not thyself because of evil men, neither be thou envious at the wicked;
verses.bsb: Do not fret over evildoers, and do not be envious of the wicked.

verses.kjv: For there shall be no reward to the evil man; the candle of the wicked shall be put out.
verses.bsb: For the evil man has no future; the lamp of the wicked will be extinguished.

verses.kjv: My son, fear thou the Lord and the king: and meddle not with them that are given to change:
verses.bsb: My son, fear the LORD and the king, and do not associate with the rebellious.

verses.kjv: For their calamity shall rise suddenly; and who knoweth the ruin of them both?
verses.bsb: For they will bring sudden destruction. Who knows what ruin they can bring?

verses.kjv: These things also belong to the wise. It is not good to have respect of persons in judgment.
verses.bsb: These also are sayings of the wise:  To show partiality in judgment is not good.

verses.kjv: He that saith unto the wicked, Thou art righteous; him shall the people curse, nations shall abhor him:
verses.bsb: Whoever tells the guilty, “You are innocent”— peoples will curse him, and nations will denounce him;

verses.kjv: But to them that rebuke him shall be delight, and a good blessing shall come upon them.
verses.bsb: but it will go well with those who convict the guilty, and rich blessing will come upon them.

verses.kjv: Every man shall kiss his lips that giveth a right answer.
verses.bsb: An honest answer given is like a kiss on the lips.

verses.kjv: Prepare thy work without, and make it fit for thyself in the field; and afterwards build thine house.
verses.bsb: Complete your outdoor work and prepare your field; after that, you may build your house.

verses.kjv: Be not a witness against thy neighbour without cause; and deceive not with thy lips.
verses.bsb: Do not testify against your neighbor without cause, and do not deceive with your lips.

verses.kjv: Say not, I will do so to him as he hath done to me: I will render to the man according to his work.
verses.bsb: Do not say, “I will do to him as he has done to me; I will repay the man according to his work.”

verses.kjv: I went by the field of the slothful, and by the vineyard of the man void of understanding;
verses.bsb: I went past the field of a slacker and by the vineyard of a man lacking judgment.

verses.kjv: And, lo, it was all grown over with thorns, and nettles had covered the face thereof, and the stone wall thereof was broken down.
verses.bsb: Thorns had grown up everywhere, thistles had covered the ground, and the stone wall was broken down.

verses.kjv: Then I saw, and considered it well: I looked upon it, and received instruction.
verses.bsb: I observed and took it to heart; I looked and received instruction:

verses.kjv: Yet a little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to sleep:
verses.bsb: A little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to rest,

verses.kjv: So shall thy poverty come as one that travelleth; and thy want as an armed man.
verses.bsb: and poverty will come upon you like a robber, and need like a bandit.

verses.kjv: THESE are also proverbs of Solomon, which the men of Hezekiah king of Judah copied out.
verses.bsb: These are additional proverbs of Solomon, which were copied by the men of Hezekiah king of Judah:

verses.kjv: It is the glory of God to conceal a thing: but the honour of kings is to search out a matter.
verses.bsb: It is the glory of God to conceal a matter and the glory of kings to search it out.

verses.kjv: The heaven for height, and the earth for depth, and the heart of kings is unsearchable.
verses.bsb: As the heavens are high and the earth is deep, so the hearts of kings cannot be searched.

verses.kjv: Take away the dross from the silver, and there shall come forth a vessel for the finer.
verses.bsb: Remove the dross from the silver, and a vessel for a silversmith will come forth.

verses.kjv: Take away the wicked from before the king, and his throne shall be established in righteousness.
verses.bsb: Remove the wicked from the king’s presence, and his throne will be established in righteousness.

verses.kjv: Put not forth thyself in the presence of the king, and stand not in the place of great men:
verses.bsb: Do not exalt yourself in the presence of the king, and do not stand in the place of great men;

verses.kjv: For better it is that it be said unto thee, Come up hither; than that thou shouldest be put lower in the presence of the prince whom thine eyes have seen.
verses.bsb: for it is better that he says to you, “Come up here!” than that you should be demoted in the presence of the prince.   Even what you have seen with your own eyes,

verses.kjv: Go not forth hastily to strive, lest thou know not what to do in the end thereof, when thy neighbour hath put thee to shame.
verses.bsb: do not bring hastily to court. Otherwise, what will you do in the end when your neighbor puts you to shame?

verses.kjv: Debate thy cause with thy neighbour himself; and discover not a secret to another:
verses.bsb: Argue your case with your neighbor without betraying another’s confidence,

verses.kjv: Lest he that heareth it put thee to shame, and thine infamy turn not away.
verses.bsb: lest the one who hears may disgrace you, and your infamy never go away.

verses.kjv: A word fitly spoken is like apples of gold in pictures of silver.
verses.bsb: A word fitly spoken is like apples of gold in settings of silver.

verses.kjv: As an earring of gold, and an ornament of fine gold, so is a wise reprover upon an obedient ear.
verses.bsb: Like an earring of gold or an ornament of fine gold is a wise man’s rebuke to a listening ear.

verses.kjv: As the cold of snow in the time of harvest, so is a faithful messenger to them that send him: for he refresheth the soul of his masters.
verses.bsb: Like the cold of snow in the time of harvest is a trustworthy messenger to those who send him; he refreshes the soul of his masters.

verses.kjv: Whoso boasteth himself of a false gift is like clouds and wind without rain.
verses.bsb: Like clouds and wind without rain is the man who boasts of gifts never given.

verses.kjv: By long forbearing is a prince persuaded, and a soft tongue breaketh the bone.
verses.bsb: Through patience a ruler can be persuaded, and a gentle tongue can break a bone.

verses.kjv: Hast thou found honey? eat so much as is sufficient for thee, lest thou be filled therewith, and vomit it.
verses.bsb: If you find honey, eat just what you need, lest you have too much and vomit it up.

verses.kjv: Withdraw thy foot from thy neighbour’s house; lest he be weary of thee, and so hate thee.
verses.bsb: Seldom set foot in your neighbor’s house, lest he grow weary and hate you.

verses.kjv: A man that beareth false witness against his neighbour is a maul, and a sword, and a sharp arrow.
verses.bsb: Like a club or sword or sharp arrow is a man who bears false witness against his neighbor.

verses.kjv: Confidence in an unfaithful man in time of trouble is like a broken tooth, and a foot out of joint.
verses.bsb: Like a broken tooth or a foot out of joint is confidence in a faithless man in time of trouble.

verses.kjv: As he that taketh away a garment in cold weather, and as vinegar upon nitre, so is he that singeth songs to an heavy heart.
verses.bsb: Like one who removes a garment on a cold day or vinegar poured on a wound is one who sings songs to a heavy heart.

verses.kjv: If thine enemy be hungry, give him bread to eat; and if he be thirsty, give him water to drink:
verses.bsb: If your enemy is hungry, give him food to eat, and if he is thirsty, give him water to drink.

verses.kjv: For thou shalt heap coals of fire upon his head, and the Lord shall reward thee.
verses.bsb: For in so doing, you will heap burning coals on his head, and the LORD will reward you.

verses.kjv: The north wind driveth away rain: so doth an angry countenance a backbiting tongue.
verses.bsb: As the north wind brings forth rain, so a backbiting tongue brings angry looks.

verses.kjv: It is better to dwell in the corner of the housetop, than with a brawling woman and in a wide house.
verses.bsb: Better to live on a corner of the roof than to share a house with a quarrelsome wife.

verses.kjv: As cold waters to a thirsty soul, so is good news from a far country.
verses.bsb: Like cold water to a weary soul is good news from a distant land.

verses.kjv: A righteous man falling down before the wicked is as a troubled fountain, and a corrupt spring.
verses.bsb: Like a muddied spring or a polluted well is a righteous man who gives way to the wicked.

verses.kjv: It is not good to eat much honey: so for men to search their own glory is not glory.
verses.bsb: It is not good to eat too much honey or to search out one’s own glory.

verses.kjv: He that hath no rule over his own spirit is like a city that is broken down, and without walls.
verses.bsb: Like a city whose walls are broken down is a man who does not control his temper.

verses.kjv: AS snow in summer, and as rain in harvest, so honour is not seemly for a fool.
verses.bsb: Like snow in summer and rain at harvest, honor does not befit a fool.

verses.kjv: As the bird by wandering, as the swallow by flying, so the curse causeless shall not come.
verses.bsb: Like a fluttering sparrow or darting swallow, an undeserved curse does not come to rest.

verses.kjv: A whip for the horse, a bridle for the ass, and a rod for the fool’s back.
verses.bsb: A whip for the horse, a bridle for the donkey, and a rod for the backs of fools!

verses.kjv: Answer not a fool according to his folly, lest thou also be like unto him.
verses.bsb: Do not answer a fool according to his folly, or you yourself will be like him.

verses.kjv: Answer a fool according to his folly, lest he be wise in his own conceit.
verses.bsb: Answer a fool according to his folly, lest he become wise in his own eyes.

verses.kjv: He that sendeth a message by the hand of a fool cutteth off the feet, and drinketh damage.
verses.bsb: Like cutting off one’s own feet or drinking violence is the sending of a message by the hand of a fool.

verses.kjv: The legs of the lame are not equal: so is a parable in the mouth of fools.
verses.bsb: Like lame legs hanging limp is a proverb in the mouth of a fool.

verses.kjv: As he that bindeth a stone in a sling, so is he that giveth honour to a fool.
verses.bsb: Like binding a stone into a sling is the giving of honor to a fool.

verses.kjv: As a thorn goeth up into the hand of a drunkard, so is a parable in the mouth of fools.
verses.bsb: Like a thorn that falls into the hand of a drunkard is a proverb in the mouth of a fool.

verses.kjv: The great God that formed all things both rewardeth the fool, and rewardeth transgressors.
verses.bsb: Like an archer who wounds at random is he who hires a fool or passerby.

verses.kjv: As a dog returneth to his vomit, so a fool returneth to his folly.
verses.bsb: As a dog returns to its vomit, so a fool repeats his folly.

verses.kjv: Seest thou a man wise in his own conceit? there is more hope of a fool than of him.
verses.bsb: Do you see a man who is wise in his own eyes? There is more hope for a fool than for him.

verses.kjv: The slothful man saith, There is a lion in the way; a lion is in the streets.
verses.bsb: The slacker says, “A lion is in the road! A fierce lion roams the public square!”

verses.kjv: As the door turneth upon his hinges, so doth the slothful upon his bed.
verses.bsb: As a door turns on its hinges, so the slacker turns on his bed.

verses.kjv: The slothful hideth his hand in his bosom; it grieveth him to bring it again to his mouth.
verses.bsb: The slacker buries his hand in the dish; it wearies him to bring it back to his mouth.

verses.kjv: The sluggard is wiser in his own conceit than seven men that can render a reason.
verses.bsb: The slacker is wiser in his own eyes than seven men who answer discreetly.

verses.kjv: He that passeth by, and meddleth with strife belonging not to him, is like one that taketh a dog by the ears.
verses.bsb: Like one who grabs a dog by the ears is a passerby who meddles in a quarrel not his own.

verses.kjv: As a mad man who casteth firebrands, arrows, and death,
verses.bsb: Like a madman shooting firebrands and deadly arrows,

verses.kjv: So is the man that deceiveth his neighbour, and saith, Am not I in sport?
verses.bsb: so is the man who deceives his neighbor and says, “I was only joking!”

verses.kjv: Where no wood is, there the fire goeth out: so where there is no talebearer, the strife ceaseth.
verses.bsb: Without wood, a fire goes out; without gossip, a conflict ceases.

verses.kjv: As coals are to burning coals, and wood to fire; so is a contentious man to kindle strife.
verses.bsb: Like charcoal for embers and wood for fire, so is a quarrelsome man for kindling strife.

verses.kjv: The words of a talebearer are as wounds, and they go down into the innermost parts of the belly.
verses.bsb: The words of a gossip are like choice morsels that go down into the inmost being.

verses.kjv: Burning lips and a wicked heart are like a potsherd covered with silver dross.
verses.bsb: Like glaze covering an earthen vessel are burning lips and a wicked heart.

verses.kjv: He that hateth dissembleth with his lips, and layeth up deceit within him;
verses.bsb: A hateful man disguises himself with his speech, but he lays up deceit in his heart.

verses.kjv: When he speaketh fair, believe him not: for there are seven abominations in his heart.
verses.bsb: When he speaks graciously, do not believe him, for seven abominations fill his heart.

verses.kjv: Whose hatred is covered by deceit, his wickedness shall be shewed before the whole congregation.
verses.bsb: Though his hatred is concealed by deception, his wickedness will be exposed in the assembly.

verses.kjv: Whoso diggeth a pit shall fall therein: and he that rolleth a stone, it will return upon him.
verses.bsb: He who digs a pit will fall into it, and he who rolls a stone will have it roll back on him.

verses.kjv: A lying tongue hateth those that are afflicted by it; and a flattering mouth worketh ruin.
verses.bsb: A lying tongue hates those it crushes, and a flattering mouth causes ruin.

verses.kjv: BOAST not thyself of to morrow; for thou knowest not what a day may bring forth.
verses.bsb: Do not boast about tomorrow, for you do not know what a day may bring.

verses.kjv: Let another man praise thee, and not thine own mouth; a stranger, and not thine own lips.
verses.bsb: Let another praise you, and not your own mouth— a stranger, and not your own lips.

verses.kjv: A stone is heavy, and the sand weighty; but a fool’s wrath is heavier than them both.
verses.bsb: A stone is heavy and sand is a burden, but aggravation from a fool outweighs them both.

verses.kjv: Wrath is cruel, and anger is outrageous; but who is able to stand before envy?
verses.bsb: Wrath is cruel and anger is like a flood, but who can withstand jealousy?

verses.kjv: Open rebuke is better than secret love.
verses.bsb: Better an open rebuke than love that is concealed.

verses.kjv: Faithful are the wounds of a friend; but the kisses of an enemy are deceitful.
verses.bsb: The wounds of a friend are faithful, but the kisses of an enemy are deceitful.

verses.kjv: The full soul loatheth an honeycomb; but to the hungry soul every bitter thing is sweet.
verses.bsb: The soul that is full loathes honey, but to a hungry soul, any bitter thing is sweet.

verses.kjv: As a bird that wandereth from her nest, so is a man that wandereth from his place.
verses.bsb: Like a bird that strays from its nest is a man who wanders from his home.

verses.kjv: Ointment and perfume rejoice the heart: so doth the sweetness of a man’s friend by hearty counsel.
verses.bsb: Oil and incense bring joy to the heart, and the sweetness of a friend is counsel to the soul.

verses.kjv: Thine own friend, and thy father’s friend, forsake not; neither go into thy brother’s house in the day of thy calamity: for better is a neighbour that is near than a brother far off.
verses.bsb: Do not forsake your friend or your father’s friend, and do not go to your brother’s house in the day of your calamity; better a neighbor nearby than a brother far away.

verses.kjv: My son, be wise, and make my heart glad, that I may answer him that reproacheth me.
verses.bsb: Be wise, my son, and bring joy to my heart, so that I can answer him who taunts me.

verses.kjv: A prudent man foreseeth the evil, and hideth himself; but the simple pass on, and are punished.
verses.bsb: The prudent see danger and take cover; but the simple keep going and pay the penalty.

verses.kjv: Take his garment that is surety for a stranger, and take a pledge of him for a strange woman.
verses.bsb: Take the garment of him who posts security for a stranger; get collateral if it is for a foreigner.

verses.kjv: He that blesseth his friend with a loud voice, rising early in the morning, it shall be counted a curse to him.
verses.bsb: If one blesses his neighbor with a loud voice early in the morning, it will be counted to him as a curse.

verses.kjv: A continual dropping in a very rainy day and a contentious woman are alike.
verses.bsb: A constant dripping on a rainy day and a contentious woman are alike—

verses.kjv: Whosoever hideth her hideth the wind, and the ointment of his right hand, which bewrayeth itself.
verses.bsb: restraining her is like holding back the wind or grasping oil with one’s right hand.

verses.kjv: Iron sharpeneth iron; so a man sharpeneth the countenance of his friend.
verses.bsb: As iron sharpens iron, so one man sharpens another.

verses.kjv: Whoso keepeth the fig tree shall eat the fruit thereof: so he that waiteth on his master shall be honoured.
verses.bsb: Whoever tends a fig tree will eat its fruit, and he who looks after his master will be honored.

verses.kjv: As in water face answereth to face, so the heart of man to man.
verses.bsb: As water reflects the face, so the heart reflects the true man.

verses.kjv: Hell and destruction are never full; so the eyes of man are never satisfied.
verses.bsb: Sheol and Abaddon are never satisfied; so the eyes of man are never satisfied.

verses.kjv: As the fining pot for silver, and the furnace for gold; so is a man to his praise.
verses.bsb: A crucible for silver and a furnace for gold, but a man is tested by the praise accorded him.

verses.kjv: Though thou shouldest bray a fool in a mortar among wheat with a pestle, yet will not his foolishness depart from him.
verses.bsb: Though you grind a fool like grain with mortar and a pestle, yet his folly will not depart from him.

verses.kjv: Be thou diligent to know the state of thy flocks, and look well to thy herds.
verses.bsb: Be sure to know the state of your flocks, and pay close attention to your herds;

verses.kjv: For riches are not for ever: and doth the crown endure to every generation?
verses.bsb: for riches are not forever, nor does a crown endure to every generation.

verses.kjv: The hay appeareth, and the tender grass sheweth itself, and herbs of the mountains are gathered.
verses.bsb: When hay is removed and new growth appears and the grain from the hills is gathered,

verses.kjv: The lambs are for thy clothing, and the goats are the price of the field.
verses.bsb: the lambs will provide you with clothing, and the goats with the price of a field.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt have goats’ milk enough for thy food, for the food of thy household, and for the maintenance for thy maidens.
verses.bsb: You will have plenty of goats’ milk to feed you— food for your household and nourishment for your maidservants.

verses.kjv: THE wicked flee when no man pursueth: but the righteous are bold as a lion.
verses.bsb: The wicked flee when no one pursues, but the righteous are as bold as a lion.

verses.kjv: For the transgression of a land many are the princes thereof: but by a man of understanding and knowledge the state thereof shall be prolonged.
verses.bsb: A land in rebellion has many rulers, but a man of understanding and knowledge maintains order.

verses.kjv: A poor man that oppresseth the poor is like a sweeping rain which leaveth no food.
verses.bsb: A destitute leader who oppresses the poor is like a driving rain that leaves no food.

verses.kjv: They that forsake the law praise the wicked: but such as keep the law contend with them.
verses.bsb: Those who forsake the law praise the wicked, but those who keep the law resist them.

verses.kjv: Evil men understand not judgment: but they that seek the Lord understand all things.
verses.bsb: Evil men do not understand justice, but those who seek the LORD comprehend fully.

verses.kjv: Better is the poor that walketh in his uprightness, than he that is perverse in his ways, though he be rich.
verses.bsb: Better a poor man who walks with integrity than a rich man whose ways are perverse.

verses.kjv: Whoso keepeth the law is a wise son: but he that is a companion of riotous men shameth his father.
verses.bsb: A discerning son keeps the law, but a companion of gluttons disgraces his father.

verses.kjv: He that by usury and unjust gain increaseth his substance, he shall gather it for him that will pity the poor.
verses.bsb: He who increases his wealth by interest and usury lays it up for one who is kind to the poor.

verses.kjv: He that turneth away his ear from hearing the law, even his prayer shall be abomination.
verses.bsb: Whoever turns his ear away from hearing the law, even his prayer is detestable.

verses.kjv: Whoso causeth the righteous to go astray in an evil way, he shall fall himself into his own pit: but the upright shall have good things in possession.
verses.bsb: He who leads the upright along the path of evil will fall into his own pit, but the blameless will inherit what is good.

verses.kjv: The rich man is wise in his own conceit; but the poor that hath understanding searcheth him out.
verses.bsb: A rich man is wise in his own eyes, but a poor man with discernment sees through him.

verses.kjv: When righteous men do rejoice, there is great glory: but when the wicked rise, a man is hidden.
verses.bsb: When the righteous triumph, there is great glory, but when the wicked rise, men hide themselves.

verses.kjv: He that covereth his sins shall not prosper: but whoso confesseth and forsaketh them shall have mercy.
verses.bsb: He who conceals his sins will not prosper, but whoever confesses and renounces them will find mercy.

verses.kjv: Happy is the man that feareth alway: but he that hardeneth his heart shall fall into mischief.
verses.bsb: Blessed is the man who is always reverent, but he who hardens his heart falls into trouble.

verses.kjv: As a roaring lion, and a ranging bear; so is a wicked ruler over the poor people.
verses.bsb: Like a roaring lion or a charging bear is a wicked ruler over a helpless people.

verses.kjv: The prince that wanteth understanding is also a great oppressor: but he that hateth covetousness shall prolong his days.
verses.bsb: A leader who lacks judgment is also a great oppressor, but he who hates dishonest profit will prolong his days.

verses.kjv: A man that doeth violence to the blood of any person shall flee to the pit; let no man stay him.
verses.bsb: A man burdened by bloodguilt will flee into the Pit; let no one support him.

verses.kjv: Whoso walketh uprightly shall be saved: but he that is perverse in his ways shall fall at once.
verses.bsb: He who walks with integrity will be kept safe, but whoever is perverse in his ways will suddenly fall.

verses.kjv: He that tilleth his land shall have plenty of bread: but he that followeth after vain persons shall have poverty enough.
verses.bsb: The one who works his land will have plenty of food, but whoever chases fantasies will have his fill of poverty.

verses.kjv: A faithful man shall abound with blessings: but he that maketh haste to be rich shall not be innocent.
verses.bsb: A faithful man will abound with blessings, but one eager to be rich will not go unpunished.

verses.kjv: To have respect of persons is not good: for for a piece of bread that man will transgress.
verses.bsb: To show partiality is not good, yet a man will do wrong for a piece of bread.

verses.kjv: He that hasteth to be rich hath an evil eye, and considereth not that poverty shall come upon him.
verses.bsb: A stingy man hastens after wealth and does not know that poverty awaits him.

verses.kjv: He that rebuketh a man afterwards shall find more favour than he that flattereth with the tongue.
verses.bsb: He who rebukes a man will later find more favor than one who flatters with his tongue.

verses.kjv: Whoso robbeth his father or his mother, and saith, It is no transgression; the same is the companion of a destroyer.
verses.bsb: He who robs his father or mother, saying, “It is not wrong,” is a companion to the man who destroys.

verses.kjv: He that is of a proud heart stirreth up strife: but he that putteth his trust in the Lord shall be made fat.
verses.bsb: A greedy man stirs up strife, but he who trusts in the LORD will prosper.

verses.kjv: He that trusteth in his own heart is a fool: but whoso walketh wisely, he shall be delivered.
verses.bsb: He who trusts in himself is a fool, but one who walks in wisdom will be safe.

verses.kjv: He that giveth unto the poor shall not lack: but he that hideth his eyes shall have many a curse.
verses.bsb: Whoever gives to the poor will not be in need, but he who hides his eyes will receive many curses.

verses.kjv: When the wicked rise, men hide themselves: but when they perish, the righteous increase.
verses.bsb: When the wicked come to power, people hide themselves; but when they perish, the righteous flourish.

verses.kjv: HE, that being often reproved hardeneth his neck, shall suddenly be destroyed, and that without remedy.
verses.bsb: A man who remains stiff-necked after much reproof will suddenly be shattered beyond recovery.

verses.kjv: When the righteous are in authority, the people rejoice: but when the wicked beareth rule, the people mourn.
