verses.bsb: When the righteous flourish, the people rejoice, but when the wicked rule, the people groan.

verses.kjv: Whoso loveth wisdom rejoiceth his father: but he that keepeth company with harlots spendeth his substance.
verses.bsb: A man who loves wisdom brings joy to his father, but a companion of prostitutes squanders his wealth.

verses.kjv: The king by judgment establisheth the land: but he that receiveth gifts overthroweth it.
verses.bsb: By justice a king brings stability to the land, but a man who exacts tribute demolishes it.

verses.kjv: A man that flattereth his neighbour spreadeth a net for his feet.
verses.bsb: A man who flatters his neighbor spreads a net for his feet.

verses.kjv: In the transgression of an evil man there is a snare: but the righteous doth sing and rejoice.
verses.bsb: An evil man is caught by his own sin, but a righteous one sings and rejoices.

verses.kjv: The righteous considereth the cause of the poor: but the wicked regardeth not to know it.
verses.bsb: The righteous consider the cause of the poor, but the wicked have no regard for such concerns.

verses.kjv: Scornful men bring a city into a snare: but wise men turn away wrath.
verses.bsb: Mockers inflame a city, but the wise turn away anger.

verses.kjv: If a wise man contendeth with a foolish man, whether he rage or laugh, there is no rest.
verses.bsb: If a wise man goes to court with a fool, there will be raving and laughing with no resolution.

verses.kjv: The bloodthirsty hate the upright: but the just seek his soul.
verses.bsb: Men of bloodshed hate a blameless man, but the upright care for his life.

verses.kjv: A fool uttereth all his mind: but a wise man keepeth it in till afterwards.
verses.bsb: A fool vents all his anger, but a wise man holds it back.

verses.kjv: If a ruler hearken to lies, all his servants are wicked.
verses.bsb: If a ruler listens to lies, all his officials will be wicked.

verses.kjv: The poor and the deceitful man meet together: the Lord lighteneth both their eyes.
verses.bsb: The poor man and the oppressor have this in common: The LORD gives light to the eyes of both.

verses.kjv: The king that faithfully judgeth the poor, his throne shall be established for ever.
verses.bsb: A king who judges the poor with fairness— his throne will be established forever.

verses.kjv: The rod and reproof give wisdom: but a child left to himself bringeth his mother to shame.
verses.bsb: A rod of correction imparts wisdom, but a child left to himself disgraces his mother.

verses.kjv: When the wicked are multiplied, transgression increaseth: but the righteous shall see their fall.
verses.bsb: When the wicked thrive, rebellion increases; but the righteous will see their downfall.

verses.kjv: Correct thy son, and he shall give thee rest; yea, he shall give delight unto thy soul.
verses.bsb: Discipline your son, and he will give you rest; he will bring delight to your soul.

verses.kjv: Where there is no vision, the people perish: but he that keepeth the law, happy is he.
verses.bsb: Where there is no vision, the people cast off restraint; but blessed is he who keeps the Law.

verses.kjv: A servant will not be corrected by words: for though he understand he will not answer.
verses.bsb: A servant cannot be corrected by words alone; though he understands, he will not respond.

verses.kjv: Seest thou a man that is hasty in his words? there is more hope of a fool than of him.
verses.bsb: Do you see a man who speaks in haste? There is more hope for a fool than for him.

verses.kjv: He that delicately bringeth up his servant from a child shall have him become his son at the length.
verses.bsb: A servant pampered from his youth will bring grief in the end.

verses.kjv: An angry man stirreth up strife, and a furious man aboundeth in transgression.
verses.bsb: An angry man stirs up dissension, and a hot-tempered man abounds in transgression.

verses.kjv: A man’s pride shall bring him low: but honour shall uphold the humble in spirit.
verses.bsb: A man’s pride will bring him low, but a humble spirit will obtain honor.

verses.kjv: Whoso is partner with a thief hateth his own soul: he heareth cursing, and bewrayeth it not.
verses.bsb: A partner to a thief hates his own soul; he receives the oath, but does not testify.

verses.kjv: The fear of man bringeth a snare: but whoso putteth his trust in the Lord shall be safe.
verses.bsb: The fear of man is a snare, but whoever trusts in the LORD is set securely on high.

verses.kjv: Many seek the ruler’s favour; but every man’s judgment cometh from the Lord.
verses.bsb: Many seek the ruler’s favor, but a man receives justice from the LORD.

verses.kjv: An unjust man is an abomination to the just: and he that is upright in the way is abomination to the wicked.
verses.bsb: An unjust man is detestable to the righteous, and one whose way is upright is detestable to the wicked.

verses.kjv: THE words of Agur the son of Jakeh, even the prophecy: the man spake unto Ithiel, even unto Ithiel and Ucal,
verses.bsb: These are the words of Agur son of Jakeh—the burden that this man declared to Ithiel:  “I am weary, O God, and worn out.

verses.kjv: Surely I am more brutish than any man, and have not the understanding of a man.
verses.bsb: Surely I am the most ignorant of men, and I lack the understanding of a man.

verses.kjv: I neither learned wisdom, nor have the knowledge of the holy.
verses.bsb: I have not learned wisdom, and I have no knowledge of the Holy One.

verses.kjv: Who hath ascended up into heaven, or descended? who hath gathered the wind in his fists? who hath bound the waters in a garment? who hath established all the ends of the earth? what is his name, and what is his son’s name, if thou canst tell?
verses.bsb: Who has ascended to heaven and come down? Who has gathered the wind in His hands? Who has bound up the waters in His cloak? Who has established all the ends of the earth? What is His name, and what is the name of His Son— surely you know!

verses.kjv: Every word of God is pure: he is a shield unto them that put their trust in him.
verses.bsb: Every word of God is flawless; He is a shield to those who take refuge in Him.

verses.kjv: Add thou not unto his words, lest he reprove thee, and thou be found a liar.
verses.bsb: Do not add to His words, lest He rebuke you and prove you a liar.

verses.kjv: Two things have I required of thee; deny me them not before I die:
verses.bsb: Two things I ask of You— do not refuse me before I die:

verses.kjv: Remove far from me vanity and lies: give me neither poverty nor riches; feed me with food convenient for me:
verses.bsb: Keep falsehood and deceitful words far from me. Give me neither poverty nor riches; feed me with the bread that is my portion.

verses.kjv: Lest I be full, and deny thee, and say, Who is the Lord? or lest I be poor, and steal, and take the name of my God in vain.
verses.bsb: Otherwise, I may have too much and deny You, saying, ‘Who is the LORD?’ Or I may become poor and steal, profaning the name of my God.

verses.kjv: Accuse not a servant unto his master, lest he curse thee, and thou be found guilty.
verses.bsb: Do not slander a servant to his master, or he will curse you, and you will bear the guilt.

verses.kjv: There is a generation that curseth their father, and doth not bless their mother.
verses.bsb: There is a generation of those who curse their fathers and do not bless their mothers.

verses.kjv: There is a generation that are pure in their own eyes, and yet is not washed from their filthiness.
verses.bsb: There is a generation of those who are pure in their own eyes and yet unwashed of their filth.

verses.kjv: There is a generation, O how lofty are their eyes! and their eyelids are lifted up.
verses.bsb: There is a generation—how haughty are their eyes and pretentious are their glances—

verses.kjv: There is a generation, whose teeth are as swords, and their jaw teeth as knives, to devour the poor from off the earth, and the needy from among men.
verses.bsb: there is a generation whose teeth are swords and whose jaws are knives, devouring the oppressed from the earth and the needy from among men.

verses.kjv: The horseleach hath two daughters, crying, Give, give. There are three things that are never satisfied, yea, four things say not, It is enough:
verses.bsb: The leech has two daughters: Give and Give.   There are three things that are never satisfied, four that never say, ‘Enough!’:

verses.kjv: The grave; and the barren womb; the earth that is not filled with water; and the fire that saith not, It is enough.
verses.bsb: Sheol,   the barren womb,   land never satisfied with water,   and fire that never says, ‘Enough!’

verses.kjv: The eye that mocketh at his father, and despiseth to obey his mother, the ravens of the valley shall pick it out, and the young eagles shall eat it.
verses.bsb: As for the eye that mocks a father and scorns obedience to a mother, may the ravens of the valley pluck it out and young vultures devour it.

verses.kjv: There be three things which are too wonderful for me, yea, four which I know not:
verses.bsb: There are three things too wonderful for me, four that I cannot understand:

verses.kjv: The way of an eagle in the air; the way of a serpent upon a rock; the way of a ship in the midst of the sea; and the way of a man with a maid.
verses.bsb: the way of an eagle in the sky,   the way of a snake on a rock,   the way of a ship at sea,   and the way of a man with a maiden.

verses.kjv: Such is the way of an adulterous woman; she eateth, and wipeth her mouth, and saith, I have done no wickedness.
verses.bsb: This is the way of an adulteress: She eats and wipes her mouth and says, ‘I have done nothing wrong.’

verses.kjv: For three things the earth is disquieted, and for four which it cannot bear:
verses.bsb: Under three things the earth trembles, under four it cannot bear up:

verses.kjv: For a servant when he reigneth; and a fool when he is filled with meat;
verses.bsb: a servant who becomes king,   a fool who is filled with food,

verses.kjv: For an odious woman when she is married; and an handmaid that is heir to her mistress.
verses.bsb: an unloved woman who marries,   and a maidservant who supplants her mistress.

verses.kjv: There be four things which are little upon the earth, but they are exceeding wise:
verses.bsb: Four things on earth are small, yet they are exceedingly wise:

verses.kjv: The ants are a people not strong, yet they prepare their meat in the summer;
verses.bsb: The ants are creatures of little strength, yet they store up their food in the summer;

verses.kjv: The conies are but a feeble folk, yet make they their houses in the rocks;
verses.bsb: the rock badgers are creatures of little power, yet they make their homes in the rocks;

verses.kjv: The locusts have no king, yet go they forth all of them by bands;
verses.bsb: the locusts have no king, yet they all advance in formation;

verses.kjv: The spider taketh hold with her hands, and is in kings’ palaces.
verses.bsb: and the lizard can be caught in one’s hands, yet it is found in the palaces of kings.

verses.kjv: There be three things which go well, yea, four are comely in going:
verses.bsb: There are three things that are stately in their stride, and four that are impressive in their walk:

verses.kjv: A lion which is strongest among beasts, and turneth not away for any;
verses.bsb: a lion, mighty among beasts, refusing to retreat before anything;

verses.kjv: A greyhound; an he goat also; and a king, against whom there is no rising up.
verses.bsb: a strutting rooster;   a he-goat;   and a king with his army around him.

verses.kjv: If thou hast done foolishly in lifting up thyself, or if thou hast thought evil, lay thine hand upon thy mouth.
verses.bsb: If you have foolishly exalted yourself or if you have plotted evil, put your hand over your mouth.

verses.kjv: Surely the churning of milk bringeth forth butter, and the wringing of the nose bringeth forth blood: so the forcing of wrath bringeth forth strife.
verses.bsb: For as the churning of milk yields butter, and the twisting of the nose draws blood, so the stirring of anger brings forth strife.”

verses.kjv: THE words of king Lemuel, the prophecy that his mother taught him.
verses.bsb: These are the words of King Lemuel—the burden that his mother taught him:

verses.kjv: What, my son? and what, the son of my womb? and what, the son of my vows?
verses.bsb: What shall I say, O my son? What, O son of my womb? What, O son of my vows?

verses.kjv: Give not thy strength unto women, nor thy ways to that which destroyeth kings.
verses.bsb: Do not spend your strength on women or your vigor on those who ruin kings.

verses.kjv: It is not for kings, O Lemuel, it is not for kings to drink wine; nor for princes strong drink:
verses.bsb: It is not for kings, O Lemuel, it is not for kings to drink wine, or for rulers to crave strong drink,

verses.kjv: Lest they drink, and forget the law, and pervert the judgment of any of the afflicted.
verses.bsb: lest they drink and forget what is decreed, depriving all the oppressed of justice.

verses.kjv: Give strong drink unto him that is ready to perish, and wine unto those that be of heavy hearts.
verses.bsb: Give strong drink to one who is perishing, and wine to the bitter in soul.

verses.kjv: Let him drink, and forget his poverty, and remember his misery no more.
verses.bsb: Let him drink and forget his poverty, and remember his misery no more.

verses.kjv: Open thy mouth for the dumb in the cause of all such as are appointed to destruction.
verses.bsb: Open your mouth for those with no voice, for the cause of all the dispossessed.

verses.kjv: Open thy mouth, judge righteously, and plead the cause of the poor and needy.
verses.bsb: Open your mouth, judge righteously, and defend the cause of the poor and needy.

verses.kjv: ¶ Who can find a virtuous woman? for her price is far above rubies.
verses.bsb: A wife of noble character, who can find? She is far more precious than rubies.

verses.kjv: The heart of her husband doth safely trust in her, so that he shall have no need of spoil.
verses.bsb: The heart of her husband trusts in her, and he lacks nothing of value.

verses.kjv: She will do him good and not evil all the days of her life.
verses.bsb: She brings him good and not harm all the days of her life.

verses.kjv: She seeketh wool, and flax, and worketh willingly with her hands.
verses.bsb: She selects wool and flax and works with eager hands.

verses.kjv: She is like the merchants’ ships; she bringeth her food from afar.
verses.bsb: She is like the merchant ships, bringing her food from afar.

verses.kjv: She riseth also while it is yet night, and giveth meat to her household, and a portion to her maidens.
verses.bsb: She rises while it is still night to provide food for her household and portions for her maidservants.

verses.kjv: She considereth a field, and buyeth it: with the fruit of her hands she planteth a vineyard.
verses.bsb: She appraises a field and buys it; from her earnings she plants a vineyard.

verses.kjv: She girdeth her loins with strength, and strengtheneth her arms.
verses.bsb: She girds herself with strength and shows that her arms are strong.

verses.kjv: She perceiveth that her merchandise is good: her candle goeth not out by night.
verses.bsb: She sees that her gain is good, and her lamp is not extinguished at night.

verses.kjv: She layeth her hands to the spindle, and her hands hold the distaff.
verses.bsb: She stretches out her hands to the distaff and grasps the spindle with her fingers.

verses.kjv: She stretcheth out her hand to the poor; yea, she reacheth forth her hands to the needy.
verses.bsb: She opens her arms to the poor and reaches out her hands to the needy.

verses.kjv: She is not afraid of the snow for her household: for all her household are clothed with scarlet.
verses.bsb: When it snows, she has no fear for her household, for they are all clothed in scarlet.

verses.kjv: She maketh herself coverings of tapestry; her clothing is silk and purple.
verses.bsb: She makes coverings for her bed; her clothing is fine linen and purple.

verses.kjv: Her husband is known in the gates, when he sitteth among the elders of the land.
verses.bsb: Her husband is known at the city gate, where he sits among the elders of the land.

verses.kjv: She maketh fine linen, and selleth it; and delivereth girdles unto the merchant.
verses.bsb: She makes linen garments and sells them; she delivers sashes to the merchants.

verses.kjv: Strength and honour are her clothing; and she shall rejoice in time to come.
verses.bsb: Strength and honor are her clothing, and she can laugh at the days to come.

verses.kjv: She openeth her mouth with wisdom; and in her tongue is the law of kindness.
verses.bsb: She opens her mouth with wisdom, and faithful instruction is on her tongue.

verses.kjv: She looketh well to the ways of her household, and eateth not the bread of idleness.
verses.bsb: She watches over the affairs of her household and does not eat the bread of idleness.

verses.kjv: Her children arise up, and call her blessed; her husband also, and he praiseth her.
verses.bsb: Her children rise up and call her blessed; her husband praises her as well:

verses.kjv: Many daughters have done virtuously, but thou excellest them all.
verses.bsb: “Many daughters have done noble things, but you surpass them all!”

verses.kjv: Favour is deceitful, and beauty is vain: but a woman that feareth the Lord, she shall be praised.
verses.bsb: Charm is deceptive and beauty is fleeting, but a woman who fears the LORD is to be praised.

verses.kjv: Give her of the fruit of her hands; and let her own works praise her in the gates.
verses.bsb: Give her the fruit of her hands, and let her works praise her at the gates.
